name: CHIP did not identify greater benefit from standard cardiovascular therapies in TIMI trials
summary: A large cardiovascular-trial genomic analysis limits the idea that CHIP status broadly predicts added benefit from standard cardiovascular drugs.
statement: Across 63,700 participants from five TIMI randomized trials, CHIP was associated with incident but not recurrent coronary events, and CHIP status did not identify greater benefit from the tested PCSK9, SGLT2, P2Y12, or FXa-directed therapies.
population_or_model: Cardiovascular trial participants with CHIP genotyping
quantitative_note: CHIP-positive status had adjusted HR 1.07 for overall cardiovascular events (95% CI 0.99 to 1.16; P=0.08), HR 1.31 for first MI (95% CI 1.05 to 1.64; P=0.02), and no significant treatment-effect heterogeneity across tested therapies.
caveats: This is not a CHIP-targeted intervention trial.
The therapies tested were standard cardiovascular mechanisms rather than clone-control or transposon-control strategies.
The result supports human disease relevance and a boundary on broad treatment extrapolation.
